title: Evaluation results
subtitle: Synthetic gate, frontier sweep, PGF constructions, and AIME — numbers from DEMO_WRITEUP and committed eval reports.

specialist_arc.heading: Coordinate / numeric gate story
specialist_arc.caption: Same synthetic gate style: near-zero on base, mid-pack after numeric fine-tunes, near-perfect once the target becomes PGF constructions. Foot-of-altitude went from ~2% to ~98% under that pivot.

frontier_sweep.heading: Frontier difficulty sweep
frontier_sweep.caption: Twelve-model pass heatmap on the numeric 800-grid. Hard cells are specific constructions plus ugly numbers; no model is reliable across the whole landscape.

images.alt: Twelve-model pass-rate heatmap on the numeric 800-grid
images.caption: Pass heatmap — models × construction difficulty cells
Op effect — which constructions drive failures

pgf.heading: PGF / construction results
pgf.caption: No dedicated “PGF heatmap” artifact — best available visuals plus utility-check pass rates. Base still scores 0% on the PGF eval, so the jump is a trained behavior.

images.alt: Base model fails, tuned specialist passes on the same scene
images.caption: Before / after — base fails, tuned passes

images.alt: Frontier LLM versus specialist SLM figure comparison
images.caption: LLM vs SLM comparison

frontier_table.heading: Frontier vs specialist (numeric 800-grid)
frontier_table.caption: Tuned specialists beat some frontier models and do not beat others. Full CSV: outputs/sweep/pass_rates.csv.

aime.heading: AIME performance
aime.caption: Compile is not the same as faithful. Distilling a frontier teacher raises compile coverage a lot while faithfulness stays much lower. Gallery thumbs are illustrator 4B v2 specialist renders.

models.heading: Trained specialists
models.caption: LoRA adapters actually trained in this project (Modal volume geotikz-outputs). Frontier API models used as judges/fallbacks are not listed here.

rows.name: qwen3-geotikz
rows.purpose: v1 numeric specialist — model invents coordinates
rows.notes: Train: train.jsonl (5,340). Gate: 46.4% on 800-grid. Early arc, not used in live copilot.

rows.name: qwen3-1.7b-geotikz
rows.purpose: v1 numeric specialist at larger base
rows.notes: Train: train.jsonl (5,340). Gate: 59.8% on 800-grid; beats gpt-4.1/gpt-4o on that grid. Eval-only; not in copilot.

rows.name: qwen3-pgf-geotikz
rows.purpose: v2 PGF construction specialist — emit calc/tkz, LaTeX does arithmetic
rows.notes: Train: train_pgf.jsonl (2,050). Gate: 98.9% on 280 PGF eval. Local CLI default; Modal copilot fallback if illustrators missing.

rows.name: qwen3-illustrator
rows.purpose: AIME/olympiad illustrator — broader constructions + distilled teacher
rows.notes: Train: illustrator_train_chat.jsonl (3,996). AIME ~69% compile / 11.3% faithful. Modal fallback after 4B.

rows.name: qwen3-illustrator-4b
rows.purpose: Larger illustrator on same v1 illustrator mix
rows.notes: Train: illustrator_train_chat.jsonl (3,996). Stronger AIME faithful (~24%). Modal fallback before 1.7B.

rows.name: qwen3-illustrator-4b-v2
rows.purpose: Current live copilot specialist — more paraphrases + harder families
rows.notes: Train: illustrator_train_chat_v2.jsonl (8,852). Promoted in Modal ADAPTERS first; ~98% expanded synthetic / ~99% paraphrase.

illustrator_training.heading: Illustrator special training
illustrator_training.caption: How the illustrator line differs from the numeric / PGF specialists: natural-language competition problems, chat-format SFT, distillation + vision filtering, then v2 paraphrase and harder-construction data.
bullets: Why it exists: the narrow PGF specialist is format-locked on held-out AIME (~14% compile). Illustrators learn broader olympiad constructions and real problem wording so they can illustrate competition geometry.

Bases: qwen3-illustrator on Qwen3-1.7B; qwen3-illustrator-4b and qwen3-illustrator-4b-v2 on Qwen3-4B. All are LoRA (r=32, α=64, dropout 0.05, all-linear), bf16, 2 epochs, effective batch 16, max_len 2560, trained on Modal A100 — same recipe for 4B v1 and v2; only the data (and adapter name) change for v2.

v1 data (illustrator_train_chat.jsonl, 3,996 chat examples): distilled real AIME/MATH geometry pairs from distill.py (gpt-5.5 teacher → compile filter → vision-judge faithfulness; Asymptote blocks stripped; held-out AIME eval sample excluded) plus GT-verified synthetic constructions across ~20 families (build_illustrator_data.py).
Chat format: each row is messages (system / user / assistant) with the construction system prompt — not the narrow numeric TikZ-only target used by qwen3-geotikz / qwen3-pgf-geotikz.

v2 data (illustrator_train_chat_v2.jsonl, 8,852): keeps the full v1 set (3,996) and adds paraphrase augmentation (3,416 frontier rewrites of synthetic prompts with TikZ targets held byte-identical) plus harder/broader constructions (1,440 from olympiad_hard.py: 12 families with 2–4 derived points, 100% round-trip validated).
v2 vs v1 results (GT gate): expanded synthetic 84.7% → 98.1%; paraphrase 98.7% → 99.1%; harder families 60% → 100% with no regression on the base 20 types (ILLUSTRATOR_4B_V2_REPORT.md).

Product role: qwen3-illustrator-4b-v2 is first in Modal ADAPTERS and the live copilot specialist. Routing prefers it for in-vocabulary constructions; out-of-vocab / too many derived points / large polygons fall back to frontier. Older illustrators and qwen3-pgf-geotikz remain fallbacks if a preferred adapter is missing on the volume.
